metadata.summary: Two Claude subagents for product scoping: MVP scope analyzer for competitor feature analysis; MLP agent with five sequential modes for upgrading MVP to Minimum Lovable Product.

content.title: Product Manager Agents
content.summary: Two Claude subagents for product scoping work: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent analyzes competitor features to recommend a minimum feature set for a new product; faion-mlp-agent upgrades an MVP to an MLP (Minimum Lovable Product) through a five-mode sequential pipeline (analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan). The MLP dimensions are Delight, Ease, Speed, Trust, and Personality.

why.p: Defining MVP scope without competitor grounding leads to either over-building (scope creep) or under-building (no differentiation). MLP work surfaces the "WOW" features that make a product memorable rather than just functional. The five-mode pipeline enforces sequencing discipline: gap analysis is meaningless without a current-state baseline, and spec updates should never happen before human review of proposed features.

list.item: Defining MVP scope for a new product where competitor analysis is needed to determine the minimum feature set
Upgrading an MVP to an MLP (Minimum Lovable Product) by identifying the "WOW" features that differentiate the product
Running a structured gap analysis between current product state and MLP targets before a roadmap planning session
Updating spec files with MLP requirements after a gap analysis or discovery session
Sequencing MLP feature implementation when multiple WOW features are identified and need to be prioritized
Product is at ideation stage only — no existing specs or MVP definition to analyze; start with discovery methodology instead
Competitor landscape is entirely novel and no comparable products exist; the MVP scope analyzer needs comparables to work from
Team is not ready to act on spec updates (e.g., mid-sprint, code freeze); run mode: analyze and mode: find-gaps for prep, defer mode: update until the team is ready
The product is B2B enterprise with highly custom requirements; cookie-cutter MLP dimensions (Delight, Ease, Speed, Trust, Personality) may not apply without modification

section.name: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent

rule.statement: Use faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent when defining MVP scope for a new product that needs competitor grounding.
rule.rationale: Competitor feature analysis provides an empirical floor for the minimum feature set. Without it, teams either under-build (no table stakes) or over-build (scope creep on non-differentiating features).

section.p: Input: product type, target market/segment, competitive context. Output: competitor feature matrix, MVP feature recommendations, scope boundary.

example.code: Task( subagent_type="faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent", prompt=( "Analyze SaaS project management tools for MVP scope. " "Target: small teams (5-15 people), focus on task tracking and collaboration." ) )

rule.statement: Run faion-mlp-agent modes in strict sequence: analyze before find-gaps, find-gaps before propose, propose before update, update before plan. Never skip a mode.
rule.rationale: Each mode's output is the required input for the next. Gap analysis without a current-state baseline produces hallucinated gaps. Spec updates without gap validation produce incorrect MLP requirements.

list.item: analyze: extract current MVP feature state from existing spec files.
find-gaps: identify gaps between current MVP and MLP across five dimensions.
propose: generate WOW feature candidates per dimension.
update: write accepted MLP requirements back to spec files.
plan: produce implementation sequence for MLP features.
Delight: micro-interactions, animations, polish.
Ease: intuitive UX, zero friction, fast onboarding.
Speed: instant feedback, fast performance.
Trust: security signals, reliability, transparency.
Personality: brand voice, memorable moments.

rule.statement: For B2B products, weight Trust and Ease higher than Delight and Personality.
rule.rationale: Enterprise buyers optimize for reliability and onboarding speed, not emotional resonance.

rule.statement: A product manager must review and select which WOW features to accept after mode:propose, before running mode:update.
rule.rationale: mode:update writes to spec files autonomously. If the agent misinterprets a gap or proposes an incorrect MLP feature, the spec is modified without a review step. Chaining propose to update without human approval produces unreviewed spec changes.

rule.statement: Ensure spec files are committed to version control before running mode:update.
rule.rationale: The agent modifies files in place. Without version control, changes are not reviewable as a diff and are effectively unauditable.

section.name: When to Use MVP Scope Analyzer First

rule.statement: For a net-new product with no existing MVP, run faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent first, then hand its output to faion-mlp-agent starting at mode:propose.
rule.rationale: There is no existing MVP to analyze on a new product, so modes analyze and find-gaps are irrelevant. The MVP scope analyzer provides the baseline that mode:propose needs.

antipattern.description: Running mode:plan without engineering review, then treating its output as a sprint backlog.
antipattern.reason: mode:plan produces an implementation sequence but does not validate technical feasibility. It does not account for team capacity, technical debt, or infrastructure constraints.

antipattern.description: Applying MLP dimensions as a rigid checklist to every product.
antipattern.reason: Not every dimension applies to every product. For some products, Speed is irrelevant; for others, Personality is counterproductive. Use dimensions as a structured brainstorm framework, not a mandatory checklist.

antipattern.description: Using faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent output without validating against current competitor research.
antipattern.reason: The agent's training data may be outdated. In fast-moving markets (AI tooling, fintech), competitor feature sets change in months.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Run faion-mlp-agent sequentially across its five modes, using the output of each mode as context for the next. A supervisor subagent coordinates the pipeline: passes the project path and mode outputs, catches errors (missing spec files, malformed JSON), and writes a session summary. For MVP scope definition on a new product, run faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent first, then hand its output to faion-mlp-agent starting at mode: propose (since there is no existing MVP to analyze).

list.item: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent — competitor feature analysis → MVP scope recommendation for new products
faion-mlp-agent (mode: analyze) — extract current MVP feature state from existing spec files
faion-mlp-agent (mode: find-gaps) — identify gaps between current MVP and MLP across 5 dimensions
faion-mlp-agent (mode: propose) — generate WOW feature candidates for each MLP dimension
faion-mlp-agent (mode: update) — write MLP requirements back to spec files
faion-mlp-agent (mode: plan) — produce implementation sequence for MLP features

example.title: Full MLP Pipeline
example.code:
from anthropic import Anthropic
project_path = "/path/to/project" product_type = "SaaS project management tool"
tasks = [ {"subagent_type": "faion-mlp-agent", "prompt": f"mode: analyze
project_path: {project_path}"}, {"subagent_type": "faion-mlp-agent", "prompt": f"mode: find-gaps
project_path: {project_path}"}, {"subagent_type": "faion-mlp-agent", "prompt": f"mode: propose
project_path: {project_path}
product_type: {product_type}"}, {"subagent_type": "faion-mlp-agent", "prompt": f"mode: update
project_path: {project_path}"}, {"subagent_type": "faion-mlp-agent", "prompt": f"mode: plan
project_path: {project_path}"}, ]

example.title: MVP Scope Analyzer Usage
example.code:
Use faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent to define MVP scope:
subagent_type: faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent prompt: | Analyze {product_type} tools for MVP scope. Target: {target_segment} Focus: {core_differentiator} Output: competitor feature matrix + minimum feature set for MVP

section.name: CLI Tools and Services
section.p: CLI tools: anthropic (Claude Agent SDK — runtime for both PM agents) is installed via pip install anthropic. linear (API) syncs MLP feature proposals to the roadmap at https://linear.app/docs/graphql. notion-client reads/write spec documents if stored in Notion via pip install notion-client.

Services: Anthropic API (SaaS) — Claude as the reasoning engine for both PM agents. Linear (SaaS) — roadmap management; MLP implementation plan syncs here. Notion (SaaS) — spec documentation; agents read/write spec files via API. Confluence (SaaS) — spec storage; API exists but writing structured content is cumbersome. Perplexity / Tavily (SaaS) — grounded competitor research for faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent.

list.item: Always run mode: analyze before mode: find-gaps — the gap analysis is meaningless without a baseline of current MVP features
Treat WOW feature proposals from mode: propose as hypotheses, not decisions — validate at least 2–3 candidates with real users before writing them to specs via mode: update
When running mode: update, ensure spec files are committed to version control first — the agent modifies files in place and changes should be reviewable as a diff
Use the MLP dimensions (Delight, Ease, Speed, Trust, Personality) as a structured brainstorm framework, not a rigid checklist — not every dimension applies to every product
For B2B products, weight Trust and Ease higher than Delight and Personality — enterprise buyers optimize for reliability and onboarding speed, not emotional resonance

mode: update writes to spec files autonomously — if the agent misinterprets a gap or proposes an incorrect MLP feature, the spec is modified without a review step; always require human review of the diff before merging

Human-in-the-loop checkpoint: after mode: propose, a product manager must review and select which WOW features to accept before running mode: update — do not chain propose → update without human approval

faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent performs competitor analysis based on training data; for fast-moving markets (AI tooling, fintech), supplement with a live web search tool call before passing outputs to the MLP pipeline
mode: plan produces an implementation sequence but does not validate technical feasibility — engineering review is required before treating the plan as a sprint backlog
faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent relies on competitor analysis that may be outdated if the tool's training data is not current; validate outputs against live competitor research
MLP dimensions are product-agnostic heuristics — they do not replace user research; WOW features proposed without user validation may miss actual delight drivers
mode: update modifies spec files; if specs are not version-controlled, changes are hard to review and roll back
mode: plan produces an implementation order but does not account for team capacity, technical debt, or infrastructure constraints — treat as input to sprint planning, not as a sprint plan
Five-mode design (analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan) assumes sequential execution; skipping steps produces lower-quality outputs in later modes

section.name: Pipeline Coordinator
section.p: Mode pipeline coordinator with error handling runs all 5 MLP modes sequentially; returns results per mode. For each mode from analyze, find-gaps, propose, update, plan, the coordinator constructs a prompt with the project path, adds product_type to the propose mode, invokes faion-mlp-agent with the prompt, stores the result, and passes context to the next mode if needed.

template.purpose: Sequential five-mode MLP pipeline coordinator with error handling
